purpose: Calibration cases for coding-feature-evaluator decisions. These fixtures are not proof evidence; they are known evaluator examples used to guard judgment drift.

cases.summary: Feature behavior is covered by a realistic API proof with durable read-back evidence, serious metadata, gate pass, and evaluator-safe scope.

inputs.feature: User can update contact status through the public API.
inputs.proof: Run API scenario, persist update, read back through public route.

cases.summary: Primary proof passes but only checks that a report file exists, while the feature claims correct rendered report contents.

inputs.feature: User receives a rendered report with correct totals.

expected_findings: Green but weak: proof can pass while report contents are wrong.

cases.summary: Source assertions, mocked readiness checks, and unit tests pass, but the configured runtime subprocess and protocol handshake central to the production-readiness claim are never activated.

inputs.feature: The configured CLI and MCP child are usable and production traffic is ready.
inputs.proof: Inspect Docker and deployment source, mock readiness helpers, and simulate subprocess error formatting without starting the configured child process.
inputs.central_manual_gap: The real configured child process and protocol handshake are not proven.

evidence_bundle.oracle_strength: source shape, monkeypatched readiness, and simulated subprocess errors

expected_findings: Central runtime activation is unproven even though proxy checks are green.
The proof admits a broken implementation can pass, so evaluator PASS is forbidden.
cases.required_next_action: repair proof to start the configured runtime boundary and read back its protocol result

cases.summary: Executor reports NEED_INPUT for a missing local CLI without checking package scripts, setup docs, or local readiness commands.

inputs.feature: Worker job completes after queue command runs.

cases.summary: Safe provider proof needs a credential that is not available after local docs, env examples, readiness checks, and safe fake target options were inspected.

inputs.feature: OAuth refresh works against a provider sandbox.

expected_findings: Credential is user-owned or external after documented recovery.
cases.required_next_action: ask for credential or safe external target
